title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Nebraska
description: Nebraska voters will elect one U.S. senator in 2026 for the Class II seat currently held by Pete Ricketts. The race matters because it will help determine control of the U.S. Senate, and Nebraska is generally a Republican-leaning state even though Dan Osborn ran competitively as an independent in 2024. The contest highlights a contrast between an incumbent Republican senator with statewide executive experience and an independent challenger emphasizing working-class and anti-establishment themes.

candidates.name: Pete Ricketts
candidates.summary: Pete Ricketts is Nebraska’s junior U.S. senator and a former two-term governor of the state. His campaign and Senate biography emphasize tax cuts, government efficiency, agriculture, and conservative policy priorities.

summary_sources.title: Pete Ricketts for Senate - Pete Delivers. Nebraskans Win.

career_history.title: U.S. Senator
career_history.description: Represents Nebraska in the U.S. Senate.

career_history.title: Governor
career_history.description: Served two terms as Nebraska governor.

career_history.title: Business executive
career_history.description: Worked in business before entering statewide elected office.

candidates.name: Dan Osborn
candidates.summary: Dan Osborn is a Navy veteran, industrial mechanic, and former labor union leader from Omaha. He is running as an independent and has framed his campaign around working-class economics, political independence, and a populist critique of both parties.

summary_sources.title: Dan Osborn | Independent for U.S. Senate
United States Senate election in Nebraska, 2026 - Ballotpedia
